cat > AGENTS.md <<'EOF'
# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization
This repository is currently uninitialized (no committed source files yet). Use this baseline structure as code is added:
- `src/`: application/source code grouped by feature or domain.
- `tests/`: automated tests mirroring `src/` paths.
- `assets/`: static files (images, templates, sample data).
- `docs/`: design notes, architecture decisions, and runbooks.

Keep modules small and cohesive. Prefer feature-oriented folders (for example, `src/email/` and `src/auth/`) over large utility dumps.

## Build, Test, and Development Commands
No build system is configured yet. After selecting a stack, add scripts and keep them consistent with this pattern:
- `npm run dev` (or equivalent): start local development mode.
- `npm test`: run the full automated test suite.
- `npm run lint`: run static checks and style rules.
- `npm run build`: produce production artifacts.

Document any non-Node alternatives in this file (for example, `make test` or `pytest`).

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
- Indentation: 2 spaces for JS/TS/JSON/YAML; 4 spaces for Python.
- File names: use `kebab-case` for files, `PascalCase` for classes/components, and `camelCase` for variables/functions.
- Keep functions focused and side effects explicit.
- Enforce formatting/linting with project tooling (for example, Prettier + ESLint, or Black + Ruff).

## Testing Guidelines
- Place tests under `tests/` and mirror source paths.
- Name tests clearly (examples: `email-service.test.ts`, `test_email_service.py`).
- Cover new logic and bug fixes with tests before merging.
- Aim for meaningful coverage on core paths; avoid brittle snapshot-only testing.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
Because there is no Git history yet, adopt Conventional Commits from the start:
- `feat: add SMTP connection manager`
- `fix: handle missing sender address`
- `chore: configure linting`

Pull requests should include:
- concise problem/solution description,
- linked issue (if available),
- test evidence (command output),
- screenshots/log samples when UI or behavior changes.

## Security & Configuration Tips
Never commit secrets. Store credentials in local env files (for example, `.env`) and provide a committed `.env.example` with placeholder values.
EOF
```

_**User (2026-02-25 17:21:55Z)**_

To build a tool on a Mac that runs on a Mac, that I can use to beautify emails in a way that it basically reflects my typical writing style. I am not a native English speaker but I have to write a lot of English emails oftentimes to colleagues where it's casual. I would love to have this built into my email client but I don't think that is a feasible way—although maybe you want to do some research and figure out if there is a way to put this into Gmail or something like that.

Generally speaking, I was thinking about using some sort of cut and paste scenario where I just write something out, get it beautified, and then just reply, copy, and paste it into my email client

cat > packages/shared/src/style-parser.js <<'EOF'
const SUPPORTED_KEYS = new Set([
  'do',
  'avoid',
  'signature_style',
  'preferred_phrases',
  'forbidden_phrases'
]);

function emptyRuleSet() {
  return {
    do: [],
    avoid: [],
    signature_style: [],
    preferred_phrases: [],
    forbidden_phrases: []
  };
}

export function parseStyleMarkdown(markdown) {
  const lines = markdown.split(/\r?\n/);
  const rules = {
    global: emptyRuleSet(),
    modes: {}
  };

  let section = 'global';

  for (const rawLine of lines) {
    const line = rawLine.trim();
    if (!line || line.startsWith('# ') || line.startsWith('###')) {
      continue;
    }

    if (line.startsWith('## ')) {
      const header = line.slice(3).trim().toLowerCase();
      if (header === 'global') {
        section = 'global';
      } else if (header.startsWith('mode:')) {
        section = header.slice(5).trim();
        if (!rules.modes[section]) {
          rules.modes[section] = emptyRuleSet();
        }
      }
      continue;
    }

    const idx = line.indexOf(':');
    if (idx === -1) {
      continue;
    }

    const key = line.slice(0, idx).trim().toLowerCase();
    const value = line.slice(idx + 1).trim();
    if (!SUPPORTED_KEYS.has(key) || !value) {
      continue;
    }

    const target = section === 'global' ? rules.global : (rules.modes[section] ??= emptyRuleSet());
    target[key].push(value);
  }

  return rules;
}

export function mergeStyleRules({ rules, mode, profile }) {
  const modeRules = rules.modes[mode] ?? emptyRuleSet();
  const merged = emptyRuleSet();

  for (const key of Object.keys(merged)) {
    merged[key] = [
      ...(profile?.[key] ?? []),
      ...rules.global[key],
      ...modeRules[key]
    ];
  }

  return merged;
}

export function styleRulesToPrompt(rules) {
  const parts = [];
  for (const [key, values] of Object.entries(rules)) {
    if (!values.length) {
      continue;
    }

    const label = key.replace('_', ' ');
    parts.push(`${label}: ${values.join('; ')}`);
  }

  return parts.join('\n');
}
EOF

cat > packages/shared/src/profile.js <<'EOF'
function averageSentenceLength(texts) {
  const sentences = texts
    .flatMap((t) => t.split(/[.!?]/g))
    .map((s) => s.trim())
    .filter(Boolean);

  if (!sentences.length) {
    return 14;
  }

  const words = sentences.reduce((total, sentence) => total + sentence.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length, 0);
  return Math.round(words / sentences.length);
}

export function buildProfileFromSamples(samples) {
  const cleaned = samples.map((s) => s.trim()).filter(Boolean);
  const avg = averageSentenceLength(cleaned);

  const profile = {
    do: [],
    avoid: [],
    signature_style: [],
    preferred_phrases: [],
    forbidden_phrases: []
  };

  if (avg <= 10) {
    profile.do.push('favor short sentences');
  } else {
    profile.do.push('use medium-length clear sentences');
  }

  const contractions = cleaned.join(' ').match(/\b(I'm|don't|can't|we're|it's|that's)\b/gi);
  if (contractions?.length) {
    profile.do.push('use contractions naturally');
  }

  profile.avoid.push('sudden tone shifts');
  return profile;
}
EOF

import os from 'node:os';
import path from 'node:path';
import { mkdir, readFile, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises';

export const EMAILBUDDY_DIR = path.join(os.homedir(), '.emailbuddy');
export const STYLE_PATH = path.join(EMAILBUDDY_DIR, 'STYLE.md');
export const CONFIG_PATH = path.join(EMAILBUDDY_DIR, 'config.json');
export const PROFILE_PATH = path.join(EMAILBUDDY_DIR, 'profile.json');
export const HISTORY_PATH = path.join(EMAILBUDDY_DIR, 'history.jsonl');

export async function ensureEmailBuddyDir() {
  await mkdir(EMAILBUDDY_DIR, { recursive: true });
}

export async function readJson(filePath, fallback) {
  try {
    const raw = await readFile(filePath, 'utf8');
    return JSON.parse(raw);
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
}

export async function writeJson(filePath, value) {
  await ensureEmailBuddyDir();
  await writeFile(filePath, JSON.stringify(value, null, 2), 'utf8');
}

export async function readText(filePath, fallback) {
  try {
    return await readFile(filePath, 'utf8');
  } catch {
    return fallback;
  }
}
EOF

import { appendFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
import {
  CONFIG_PATH,
  HISTORY_PATH,
  PROFILE_PATH,
  STYLE_PATH,
  ensureEmailBuddyDir,
  readJson,
  readText,
  writeJson
} from './file-utils.js';

const DEFAULT_CONFIG = {
  host: '127.0.0.1',
  port: 48123,
  providerOrder: ['openai', 'anthropic', 'ollama'],
  history: {
    enabled: false
  },
  timeoutMs: 12000
};

const DEFAULT_STYLE = `# EmailBuddy Style Configuration\n\n## global\n\ndo: keep language clear and natural for non-native English writer\navoid: overly formal phrases and corporate jargon\n\n## mode: casual\n\ndo: sound warm and collaborative\n\n## mode: polished\n\ndo: improve grammar and sentence flow\n\n## mode: concise\n\ndo: reduce unnecessary words\n`;

export async function loadConfig() {
  await ensureEmailBuddyDir();
  return readJson(CONFIG_PATH, DEFAULT_CONFIG);
}

export async function saveConfig(nextConfig) {
  const merged = {
    ...DEFAULT_CONFIG,
    ...nextConfig,
    history: {
      ...DEFAULT_CONFIG.history,
      ...(nextConfig.history ?? {})
    }
  };

  await writeJson(CONFIG_PATH, merged);
  return merged;
}

export async function loadStyleMarkdown() {
  return readText(STYLE_PATH, DEFAULT_STYLE);
}

export async function loadProfile() {
  return readJson(PROFILE_PATH, null);
}

export async function saveProfile(profile) {
  await writeJson(PROFILE_PATH, profile);
  return profile;
}

export async function appendHistory(item) {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  if (!config.history.enabled) {
    return;
  }

  await ensureEmailBuddyDir();
  await appendFile(HISTORY_PATH, `${JSON.stringify(item)}\n`, 'utf8');
}
EOF

import { execFile } from 'node:child_process';
import { promisify } from 'node:util';

const execFileAsync = promisify(execFile);
const SERVICE = 'emailbuddy';

export async function setSecret(account, value) {
  await execFileAsync('security', [
    'add-generic-password',
    '-U',
    '-a',
    account,
    '-s',
    SERVICE,
    '-w',
    value
  ]);
}

export async function getSecret(account) {
  try {
    const { stdout } = await execFileAsync('security', [
      'find-generic-password',
      '-a',
      account,
      '-s',
      SERVICE,
      '-w'
    ]);
    return stdout.trim();
  } catch {
    return '';
  }
}

cat > apps/companion/src/providers.js <<'EOF'
import { getSecret } from './keychain.js';

async function parseJsonOrThrow(response) {
  const body = await response.text();
  if (!response.ok) {
    throw new Error(`Provider error ${response.status}: ${body.slice(0, 300)}`);
  }

  return JSON.parse(body);
}

function rewriteSystemPrompt({ mode, rulesPrompt }) {
  return [
    'You are an email rewriting assistant.',
    `Mode: ${mode}`,
    'Rewrite the email in natural English while preserving intent and facts.',
    'Avoid introducing new commitments or changing meaning.',
    'Respect style directives below:',
    rulesPrompt
  ].join('\n');
}

export class OpenAIProvider {
  name = 'openai';

  async rewrite({ text, mode, rulesPrompt, timeoutMs }) {
    const apiKey = await getSecret('openai_api_key');
    if (!apiKey) {
      throw new Error('Missing OpenAI API key in macOS keychain (account: openai_api_key).');
    }

    const controller = new AbortController();
    const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
    try {
      const response = await fetch('https://api.openai.com/v1/responses', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`
        },
        signal: controller.signal,
        body: JSON.stringify({
          model: 'gpt-4.1-mini',
          input: [
            { role: 'system', content: rewriteSystemPrompt({ mode, rulesPrompt }) },
            { role: 'user', content: text }
          ],
          temperature: 0.4
        })
      });

      const json = await parseJsonOrThrow(response);
      const output = json.output_text?.trim();
      if (!output) {
        throw new Error('OpenAI response missing output_text');
      }

      return output;
    } finally {
      clearTimeout(timer);
    }
  }
}

export class AnthropicProvider {
  name = 'anthropic';

  async rewrite({ text, mode, rulesPrompt, timeoutMs }) {
    const apiKey = await getSecret('anthropic_api_key');
    if (!apiKey) {
      throw new Error('Missing Anthropic API key in macOS keychain (account: anthropic_api_key).');
    }

    const controller = new AbortController();
    const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
    try {
      const response = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          'x-api-key': apiKey,
          'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01'
        },
        signal: controller.signal,
        body: JSON.stringify({
          model: 'claude-3-5-haiku-latest',
          max_tokens: 1200,
          temperature: 0.4,
          system: rewriteSystemPrompt({ mode, rulesPrompt }),
          messages: [{ role: 'user', content: text }]
        })
      });

      const json = await parseJsonOrThrow(response);
      const output = json.content?.find((c) => c.type === 'text')?.text?.trim();
      if (!output) {
        throw new Error('Anthropic response missing text content');
      }

      return output;
    } finally {
      clearTimeout(timer);
    }
  }
}

export class OllamaProvider {
  name = 'ollama';

  async rewrite({ text, mode, rulesPrompt, timeoutMs }) {
    const controller = new AbortController();
    const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
    try {
      const response = await fetch('http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        signal: controller.signal,
        body: JSON.stringify({
          model: 'llama3.1:8b',
          stream: false,
          prompt: `${rewriteSystemPrompt({ mode, rulesPrompt })}\n\nEmail:\n${text}\n\nRewritten email:`
        })
      });

      const json = await parseJsonOrThrow(response);
      const output = json.response?.trim();
      if (!output) {
        throw new Error('Ollama response missing response text');
      }

      return output;
    } finally {
      clearTimeout(timer);
    }
  }
}

export function buildProviderRegistry() {
  return {
    openai: new OpenAIProvider(),
    anthropic: new AnthropicProvider(),
    ollama: new OllamaProvider()
  };
}
EOF

cat > apps/companion/src/rewrite.js <<'EOF'
import { mergeStyleRules, parseStyleMarkdown, styleRulesToPrompt } from '../../../packages/shared/src/style-parser.js';
import { buildProfileFromSamples } from '../../../packages/shared/src/profile.js';
import { normalizeMode } from '../../../packages/shared/src/types.js';
import { appendHistory, loadConfig, loadProfile, loadStyleMarkdown, saveProfile } from './config.js';
import { buildProviderRegistry } from './providers.js';

const providerRegistry = buildProviderRegistry();

export async function rewriteEmail(request) {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  const styleMarkdown = await loadStyleMarkdown();
  const parsedRules = parseStyleMarkdown(styleMarkdown);
  const profile = await loadProfile();

  const mode = normalizeMode(request.mode);
  const mergedRules = mergeStyleRules({
    rules: parsedRules,
    mode,
    profile
  });

  const rulesPrompt = styleRulesToPrompt(mergedRules);
  const attempts = [];

  for (const providerName of config.providerOrder) {
    const provider = providerRegistry[providerName];
    if (!provider) {
      continue;
    }

    try {
      const rewrittenText = await provider.rewrite({
        text: request.text,
        mode,
        rulesPrompt,
        timeoutMs: config.timeoutMs
      });

      await appendHistory({
        ts: new Date().toISOString(),
        mode,
        provider: providerName,
        text: request.text,
        rewrittenText
      });

      return {
        rewrittenText,
        appliedMode: mode,
        providerUsed: providerName,
        notes: attempts
      };
    } catch (error) {
      attempts.push(`${providerName}: ${error.message}`);
    }
  }

  throw new Error(`All providers failed. ${attempts.join(' | ')}`);
}

export async function buildProfile(samples) {
  const profile = buildProfileFromSamples(samples);
  await saveProfile(profile);
  return profile;
}
EOF

cat > apps/companion/src/index.js <<'EOF'
import http from 'node:http';
import { URL } from 'node:url';
import { loadConfig, saveConfig } from './config.js';
import { setSecret } from './keychain.js';
import { buildProfile, rewriteEmail } from './rewrite.js';

function json(res, code, body) {
  res.writeHead(code, {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
    'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type',
    'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET,POST,PUT,OPTIONS'
  });
  res.end(JSON.stringify(body));
}

function notFound(res) {
  json(res, 404, { error: 'Not found' });
}

async function readBody(req) {
  const chunks = [];
  for await (const chunk of req) {
    chunks.push(chunk);
  }
  const raw = Buffer.concat(chunks).toString('utf8');
  if (!raw) {
    return {};
  }
  return JSON.parse(raw);
}

async function handler(req, res) {
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    return json(res, 204, {});
  }

  const url = new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost');

  try {
    if (req.method === 'GET' && url.pathname === '/v1/health') {
      return json(res, 200, { ok: true });
    }

    if (req.method === 'POST' && url.pathname === '/v1/rewrite') {
      const body = await readBody(req);
      if (!body.text || typeof body.text !== 'string') {
        return json(res, 400, { error: 'text is required' });
      }

      const result = await rewriteEmail(body);
      return json(res, 200, result);
    }

    if (req.method === 'POST' && url.pathname === '/v1/profile/samples') {
      const body = await readBody(req);
      if (!Array.isArray(body.samples)) {
        return json(res, 400, { error: 'samples must be an array of strings' });
      }

      const profile = await buildProfile(body.samples);
      return json(res, 200, { profile });
    }

    if (req.method === 'GET' && url.pathname === '/v1/config') {
      const config = await loadConfig();
      return json(res, 200, config);
    }

    if (req.method === 'PUT' && url.pathname === '/v1/config') {
      const body = await readBody(req);
      const config = await saveConfig(body);
      return json(res, 200, config);
    }

    if (req.method === 'POST' && url.pathname === '/v1/secrets') {
      const body = await readBody(req);
      if (!body.account || !body.value) {
        return json(res, 400, { error: 'account and value are required' });
      }

      await setSecret(body.account, body.value);
      return json(res, 200, { ok: true });
    }

    return notFound(res);
  } catch (error) {
    return json(res, 500, { error: error.message });
  }
}

const config = await loadConfig();
const server = http.createServer(handler);

server.listen(config.port, config.host, () => {
  console.log(`EmailBuddy companion listening on http://${config.host}:${config.port}`);
});
EOF

cat > apps/extension/src/content.js <<'EOF'
const API_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:48123/v1/rewrite';
const TOOLBAR_ATTR = 'data-emailbuddy-toolbar';

function activeCompose() {
  return document.querySelector('div[aria-label="Message Body"][contenteditable="true"]')
    || document.querySelector('div[role="textbox"][contenteditable="true"]');
}

function getSelectedTextWithin(element) {
  const selection = window.getSelection();
  if (!selection || !selection.rangeCount) {
    return '';
  }

  const range = selection.getRangeAt(0);
  if (!element.contains(range.commonAncestorContainer)) {
    return '';
  }

  return selection.toString();
}

function replaceSelectionWithin(element, text) {
  const selection = window.getSelection();
  if (selection && selection.rangeCount) {
    const range = selection.getRangeAt(0);
    if (element.contains(range.commonAncestorContainer) && !range.collapsed) {
      range.deleteContents();
      range.insertNode(document.createTextNode(text));
      selection.removeAllRanges();
      return true;
    }
  }

  element.focus();
  document.execCommand('selectAll', false);
  document.execCommand('insertText', false, text);
  return true;
}

async function beautify(composeEl, mode, customMode, statusEl, buttonEl) {
  const selected = getSelectedTextWithin(composeEl);
  const sourceText = selected || composeEl.innerText;

  if (!sourceText.trim()) {
    statusEl.textContent = 'No text found in compose box.';
    return;
  }

  buttonEl.disabled = true;
  statusEl.textContent = 'Rewriting...';

  try {
    const appliedMode = mode === 'custom' ? (customMode || 'casual') : mode;
    const response = await fetch(API_URL, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        text: sourceText,
        mode: appliedMode
      })
    });

    const body = await response.json();
    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(body.error || 'Rewrite failed');
    }

    replaceSelectionWithin(composeEl, body.rewrittenText);
    statusEl.textContent = `Done via ${body.providerUsed}.`;
  } catch (error) {
    statusEl.textContent = `Error: ${error.message}`;
  } finally {
    buttonEl.disabled = false;
  }
}

function createToolbar(composeEl) {
  const existing = composeEl.parentElement?.querySelector(`[${TOOLBAR_ATTR}]`);
  if (existing) {
    return;
  }

  const toolbar = document.createElement('div');
  toolbar.className = 'emailbuddy-toolbar';
  toolbar.setAttribute(TOOLBAR_ATTR, '1');

  const modeSelect = document.createElement('select');
  modeSelect.innerHTML = `
    <option value="casual">casual</option>
    <option value="polished">polished</option>
    <option value="concise">concise</option>
    <option value="custom">custom</option>
  `;

  const customInput = document.createElement('input');
  customInput.placeholder = 'custom mode';
  customInput.style.display = 'none';

  modeSelect.addEventListener('change', () => {
    customInput.style.display = modeSelect.value === 'custom' ? 'inline-block' : 'none';
  });

  const button = document.createElement('button');
  button.type = 'button';
  button.textContent = 'Beautify';

  const status = document.createElement('span');
  status.className = 'emailbuddy-status';

  button.addEventListener('click', () => {
    beautify(composeEl, modeSelect.value, customInput.value.trim(), status, button);
  });

  toolbar.append(modeSelect, customInput, button, status);

  const actionBar = composeEl.closest('[role="dialog"]')?.querySelector('[aria-label="More send options"]')?.parentElement;
  if (actionBar) {
    actionBar.appendChild(toolbar);
  } else {
    composeEl.parentElement?.appendChild(toolbar);
  }
}

function initObserver() {
  const observer = new MutationObserver(() => {
    const composeEl = activeCompose();
    if (composeEl) {
      createToolbar(composeEl);
    }
  });

  observer.observe(document.body, { childList: true, subtree: true });
}

document.addEventListener('keydown', (event) => {
  if (!(event.metaKey && event.shiftKey && event.key.toLowerCase() === 'b')) {
    return;
  }

  const composeEl = activeCompose();
  if (!composeEl) {
    return;
  }

  const toolbar = composeEl.parentElement?.querySelector(`[${TOOLBAR_ATTR}]`);
  const mode = toolbar?.querySelector('select')?.value ?? 'casual';
  const custom = toolbar?.querySelector('input')?.value?.trim() ?? '';
  const status = toolbar?.querySelector('.emailbuddy-status') ?? { textContent: '' };
  const button = toolbar?.querySelector('button') ?? { disabled: false };

  event.preventDefault();
  beautify(composeEl, mode, custom, status, button);
});

initObserver();
EOF
```

cat > tests/shared/style-parser.test.js <<'EOF'
import test from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
import {
  mergeStyleRules,
  parseStyleMarkdown,
  styleRulesToPrompt
} from '../../packages/shared/src/style-parser.js';

const STYLE_MD = `
## global

do: be clear
avoid: buzzwords

## mode: casual

do: be warm
`;

test('parseStyleMarkdown parses global and mode sections', () => {
  const parsed = parseStyleMarkdown(STYLE_MD);
  assert.deepEqual(parsed.global.do, ['be clear']);
  assert.deepEqual(parsed.global.avoid, ['buzzwords']);
  assert.deepEqual(parsed.modes.casual.do, ['be warm']);
});

test('mergeStyleRules applies profile then global then mode precedence', () => {
  const parsed = parseStyleMarkdown(STYLE_MD);
  const merged = mergeStyleRules({
    rules: parsed,
    mode: 'casual',
    profile: {
      do: ['use contractions'],
      avoid: ['legalese'],
      signature_style: [],
      preferred_phrases: [],
      forbidden_phrases: []
    }
  });

  assert.deepEqual(merged.do, ['use contractions', 'be clear', 'be warm']);
  assert.deepEqual(merged.avoid, ['legalese', 'buzzwords']);
});

test('styleRulesToPrompt renders only populated directives', () => {
  const prompt = styleRulesToPrompt({
    do: ['be clear'],
    avoid: [],
    signature_style: ['short close'],
    preferred_phrases: [],
    forbidden_phrases: []
  });

  assert.match(prompt, /do: be clear/);
  assert.match(prompt, /signature style: short close/);
  assert.doesNotMatch(prompt, /avoid:/);
});
EOF

cat > tests/companion/rewrite.test.js <<'EOF'
import test from 'node:test';
import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
import { rewriteEmail } from '../../apps/companion/src/rewrite.js';

const styleMarkdown = `
## global

do: be concise
`;

test('rewriteEmail falls back to later provider when first fails', async () => {
  const providerRegistry = {
    openai: {
      async rewrite() {
        throw new Error('simulated outage');
      }
    },
    mock: {
      async rewrite({ text }) {
        return `rewritten:${text}`;
      }
    }
  };

  const result = await rewriteEmail(
    { text: 'hello team', mode: 'casual' },
    {
      config: {
        providerOrder: ['openai', 'mock'],
        timeoutMs: 100,
        history: { enabled: false }
      },
      styleMarkdown,
      profile: null,
      providerRegistry,
      skipHistory: true
    }
  );

  assert.equal(result.providerUsed, 'mock');
  assert.equal(result.rewrittenText, 'rewritten:hello team');
  assert.equal(result.appliedMode, 'casual');
  assert.equal(result.notes.length, 1);
  assert.match(result.notes[0], /openai: simulated outage/);
});
EOF

```bash
cat > README.md <<'EOF'
# EmailBuddy

EmailBuddy is a macOS-focused toolchain for beautifying Gmail draft text in your personal style.

## Components
- `apps/extension`: Chrome extension that injects a `Beautify` control into Gmail compose.
- `apps/companion`: Local HTTP service (`127.0.0.1:48123`) that rewrites text with cloud-first + local fallback.
- `packages/shared`: Style/profile parsing and common helpers.

## Quick Start
1. Start the companion service:
```bash
npm run dev
```
2. Add cloud keys to macOS Keychain (optional but recommended):
```bash
security add-generic-password -U -a openai_api_key -s emailbuddy -w '<OPENAI_KEY>'
security add-generic-password -U -a anthropic_api_key -s emailbuddy -w '<ANTHROPIC_KEY>'
```
3. Ensure local fallback is available:
```bash
ollama serve
ollama pull llama3.1:8b
```
4. Load extension in Chrome:
- Open `chrome://extensions`
- Enable Developer Mode
- Click **Load unpacked** and select `apps/extension/src`

## Style Configuration
Edit `~/.emailbuddy/STYLE.md` to define mode behavior:

```md
## mode: casual
do: sound warm and collaborative
avoid: stiff wording
```

Markdown rules override learned profile traits.

## API
- `POST /v1/rewrite`
- `POST /v1/profile/samples`
- `GET/PUT /v1/config`
- `POST /v1/secrets`

## Test and Build
```bash
npm test
npm run build
```
EOF
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-26 21:20:34Z)**_

I found a likely root cause: the content script watches the entire Gmail DOM and runs a full compose lookup on every mutation, which is very frequent in Gmail and can slow compose interactions. I’m going to patch that with throttling and targeted compose detection, then add `ARCHITECTURE.md` with service-call timing.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command**</summary>
```bash
cat > ARCHITECTURE.md <<'EOF'
# EmailBuddy Architecture

## Overview
EmailBuddy has two runtime components:
1. **Chrome extension** in Gmail (`apps/extension/src/content.js`)
2. **Local companion service** on macOS (`apps/companion/src/index.js`)

The extension handles UI and text selection in Gmail. The companion app handles style/profile loading, provider routing, and model calls.

## Component Responsibilities
- **Extension (MV3 content script)**
  - Detects Gmail compose editors.
  - Injects toolbar controls (mode select + Beautify button).
  - Reads selected draft text (or full compose text).
  - Calls local API and replaces the draft text with rewritten output.

- **Companion service (Node HTTP server)**
  - Exposes `POST /v1/rewrite`, `POST /v1/profile/samples`, `GET/PUT /v1/config`, `POST /v1/secrets`.
  - Reads `~/.emailbuddy/STYLE.md` and optional `profile.json`.
  - Merges style/profile directives and builds prompt constraints.
  - Tries providers in configured order (cloud first, local Ollama fallback).

## Rewrite Request Flow
1. User clicks **Beautify** (or `Cmd+Shift+B`) in Gmail compose.
2. Extension sends `POST http://127.0.0.1:48123/v1/rewrite` with:
   - `text`
   - `mode` (`casual`, `polished`, `concise`, or custom)
3. Companion:
   - parses style markdown,
   - resolves mode,
   - attempts providers in `config.providerOrder`.
4. First successful provider returns:
   - `rewrittenText`, `appliedMode`, `providerUsed`, `notes`.
5. Extension replaces selected/compose text and shows provider status.

## When Extension Calls Companion
- **On Beautify button click**: one rewrite API call.
- **On keyboard shortcut (`Cmd+Shift+B`)**: one rewrite API call.
- No background polling is used.
- Config/profile endpoints are available for future settings UI and setup scripts.

## Performance Notes
Gmail mutates the DOM frequently. The extension now throttles toolbar refresh with `requestAnimationFrame` and only reacts to added nodes that look like compose editors, reducing overhead on compose interactions.
EOF

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-26 21:21:02Z)**_

You’re seeing slowness because Gmail’s DOM changes constantly, and the original extension was doing work on **every mutation**:

- It observed `document.body` with `subtree: true` and ran compose detection each time ([content.js](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/apps/extension/src/content.js#L125)).
- In Gmail, that callback can fire very frequently, so compose interactions feel laggy.

I implemented a performance fix:

- Added mutation filtering so we only react when added nodes look like compose editors.
- Added `requestAnimationFrame` throttling so toolbar refresh runs at most once per frame.
- Kept behavior unchanged for rewrite actions.

Updated file:
- [content.js](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/apps/extension/src/content.js)

I also created the architecture document you asked for:
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/ARCHITECTURE.md)

It includes:
- component breakdown (extension vs companion),
- end-to-end rewrite flow,
- exactly when the extension calls companion APIs,
- performance notes.

Validation:
- `npm test` passes (4/4).

Next step: reload the unpacked extension in Chrome (`chrome://extensions`) so the performance fix takes effect.

---

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-26 21:39:43Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Recommending manual trigger options**
</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-26 21:39:46Z)**_

Not the best approach for your goal.

For your use case, the best UX/perf tradeoff is:

1. **Manual trigger only (recommended)**
- No Gmail DOM observer at all.
- You select text in compose, press a shortcut, extension rewrites selection.
- Fastest and least fragile.

2. **Context-menu action**
- Right-click selected text → “Beautify with EmailBuddy”.
- Very stable, zero background DOM work.

3. **Popup workflow**
- Click extension icon, paste text, beautify, copy back.
- Most robust, but slower UX than shortcut-on-selection.

Your current version already uses a button and shortcut, but it still injects UI via DOM observation, which is the expensive part in Gmail.  
If you want, I can refactor it now to **shortcut/context-menu only** and remove all continuous DOM watching.

---

_**User (2026-02-26 21:43:07Z)**_

---

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-26 21:43:13Z)**_

Yes. By “press a shortcut” I mean a key combination, like `Cmd+Shift+B` on macOS.

     1	# EmailBuddy
     2	
     3	EmailBuddy is a macOS-focused toolchain for beautifying Gmail draft text in your personal style.
     4	
     5	## Components
     6	- `apps/extension`: Chrome extension that injects a `Beautify` control into Gmail compose.
     7	- `apps/companion`: Local HTTP service (`127.0.0.1:48123`) that rewrites text with cloud-first + local fallback.
     8	- `packages/shared`: Style/profile parsing and common helpers.
     9	
    10	## Quick Start
    11	1. Start the companion service:
    12	```bash
    13	npm run dev
    14	```
    15	2. Add cloud keys to macOS Keychain (optional but recommended):
    16	```bash
    17	security add-generic-password -U -a openai_api_key -s emailbuddy -w '<OPENAI_KEY>'
    18	security add-generic-password -U -a anthropic_api_key -s emailbuddy -w '<ANTHROPIC_KEY>'
    19	```
    20	3. Ensure local fallback is available:
    21	```bash
    22	ollama serve
    23	ollama pull llama3.1:8b
    24	```
    25	4. Load extension in Chrome:
    26	- Open `chrome://extensions`
    27	- Enable Developer Mode
    28	- Click **Load unpacked** and select `apps/extension/src`
    29	
    30	## Style Configuration
    31	Edit `~/.emailbuddy/STYLE.md` to define mode behavior:
    32	
    33	```md
    34	## mode: casual
    35	do: sound warm and collaborative
    36	avoid: stiff wording
    37	```
    38	
    39	Markdown rules override learned profile traits.
    40	
    41	## API
    42	- `POST /v1/rewrite`
    43	- `POST /v1/profile/samples`
    44	- `GET/PUT /v1/config`
    45	- `POST /v1/secrets`
    46	
    47	## Test and Build
    48	```bash
    49	npm test
    50	npm run build
    51	```
    52	
    53	## Optional: Auto-start Companion at Login
    54	1. Copy and edit [docs/com.emailbuddy.companion.plist](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/docs/com.emailbuddy.companion.plist) (replace `USERNAME` path).
    55	2. Install it:
    56	```bash
    57	cp docs/com.emailbuddy.companion.plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
    58	launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.emailbuddy.companion.plist
    59	```
```
</details></tool-use>

Output:
     1	# EmailBuddy Architecture
     2	
     3	## Overview
     4	EmailBuddy has two runtime components:
     5	1. **Chrome extension** in Gmail (`apps/extension/src/content.js`)
     6	2. **Local companion service** on macOS (`apps/companion/src/index.js`)
     7	
     8	The extension handles UI and text selection in Gmail. The companion app handles style/profile loading, provider routing, and model calls.
     9	
    10	## Component Responsibilities
    11	- **Extension (MV3 content script)**
    12	  - Detects Gmail compose editors.
    13	  - Injects toolbar controls (mode select + Beautify button).
    14	  - Reads selected draft text (or full compose text).
    15	  - Calls local API and replaces the draft text with rewritten output.
    16	
    17	- **Companion service (Node HTTP server)**
    18	  - Exposes `POST /v1/rewrite`, `POST /v1/profile/samples`, `GET/PUT /v1/config`, `POST /v1/secrets`.
    19	  - Reads `~/.emailbuddy/STYLE.md` and optional `profile.json`.
    20	  - Merges style/profile directives and builds prompt constraints.
    21	  - Tries providers in configured order (cloud first, local Ollama fallback).
    22	
    23	## Rewrite Request Flow
    24	1. User clicks **Beautify** (or `Cmd+Shift+B`) in Gmail compose.
    25	2. Extension sends `POST http://127.0.0.1:48123/v1/rewrite` with:
    26	   - `text`
    27	   - `mode` (`casual`, `polished`, `concise`, or custom)
    28	3. Companion:
    29	   - parses style markdown,
    30	   - resolves mode,
    31	   - attempts providers in `config.providerOrder`.
    32	4. First successful provider returns:
    33	   - `rewrittenText`, `appliedMode`, `providerUsed`, `notes`.
    34	5. Extension replaces selected/compose text and shows provider status.
    35	
    36	## When Extension Calls Companion
    37	- **On Beautify button click**: one rewrite API call.
    38	- **On keyboard shortcut (`Cmd+Shift+B`)**: one rewrite API call.
    39	- No background polling is used.
    40	- Config/profile endpoints are available for future settings UI and setup scripts.
    41	
    42	## Performance Notes
    43	Gmail mutates the DOM frequently. The extension now throttles toolbar refresh with `requestAnimationFrame` and only reacts to added nodes that look like compose editors, reducing overhead on compose interactions.
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-26 21:43:48Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command**</summary>
```bash
cat > apps/extension/src/content.js <<'EOF'
const API_URL = 'http://127.0.0.1:48123/v1/rewrite';
const DEFAULT_MODE = 'casual';

function activeCompose() {
  return document.querySelector('div[aria-label="Message Body"][contenteditable="true"]')
    || document.querySelector('div[role="textbox"][contenteditable="true"]');
}

function getSelectedTextWithin(element) {
  const selection = window.getSelection();
  if (!selection || !selection.rangeCount) {
    return '';
  }

  const range = selection.getRangeAt(0);
  if (!element.contains(range.commonAncestorContainer)) {
    return '';
  }

  return selection.toString();
}

function replaceSelectionWithin(element, text) {
  const selection = window.getSelection();
  if (selection && selection.rangeCount) {
    const range = selection.getRangeAt(0);
    if (element.contains(range.commonAncestorContainer) && !range.collapsed) {
      range.deleteContents();
      range.insertNode(document.createTextNode(text));
      selection.removeAllRanges();
      return;
    }
  }

  element.focus();
  document.execCommand('selectAll', false);
  document.execCommand('insertText', false, text);
}

function showToast(message, isError = false) {
  const existing = document.getElementById('emailbuddy-toast');
  if (existing) {
    existing.remove();
  }

  const toast = document.createElement('div');
  toast.id = 'emailbuddy-toast';
  toast.className = `emailbuddy-toast${isError ? ' error' : ''}`;
  toast.textContent = message;
  document.body.appendChild(toast);

  setTimeout(() => {
    toast.remove();
  }, 2200);
}

async function beautifyFromShortcut() {
  const composeEl = activeCompose();
  if (!composeEl) {
    showToast('No active Gmail compose found.', true);
    return;
  }

  const selected = getSelectedTextWithin(composeEl);
  const sourceText = selected || composeEl.innerText;
  if (!sourceText.trim()) {
    showToast('No text found in compose box.', true);
    return;
  }

  showToast('Rewriting...');

  try {
    const response = await fetch(API_URL, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        text: sourceText,
        mode: DEFAULT_MODE
      })
    });

    const body = await response.json();
    if (!response.ok) {
      throw new Error(body.error || 'Rewrite failed');
    }

    replaceSelectionWithin(composeEl, body.rewrittenText);
    showToast(`Done via ${body.providerUsed}.`);
  } catch (error) {
    showToast(`EmailBuddy error: ${error.message}`, true);
  }
}

document.addEventListener('keydown', (event) => {
  if (!(event.metaKey && event.shiftKey && event.key.toLowerCase() === 'b')) {
    return;
  }

  if (!(event.target instanceof HTMLElement) || !event.target.closest('div[role="textbox"], div[contenteditable="true"]')) {
    return;
  }

  event.preventDefault();
  beautifyFromShortcut();
});
EOF
```

        <label><input type="checkbox" id="ctrl" /> Ctrl</label>
        <label><input type="checkbox" id="alt" /> Alt</label>
        <label><input type="checkbox" id="shift" checked /> Shift</label>

      </form>

cat > apps/extension/src/options.css <<'EOF'
body {
  font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif;
  color: #222;
  margin: 24px;
}

main {
  max-width: 560px;
}

h1 {
  margin: 0 0 8px;
}

form {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(4, auto);
  gap: 10px 14px;
  align-items: center;
  margin: 18px 0;
}

.key-label {
  grid-column: 1 / span 1;
}

  width: 64px;
  padding: 6px;
  border: 1px solid #ccc;
  border-radius: 6px;
}

  padding: 8px 12px;

  background: #e8f0fe;

EOF

cat > apps/extension/src/options.js <<'EOF'
const SHORTCUT_KEY = 'shortcut';
const DEFAULT_SHORTCUT = 'Meta+Shift+E';

const metaEl = document.getElementById('meta');
const ctrlEl = document.getElementById('ctrl');
const altEl = document.getElementById('alt');
const shiftEl = document.getElementById('shift');
const keyEl = document.getElementById('key');
const formEl = document.getElementById('shortcut-form');
const statusEl = document.getElementById('status');

function parseShortcut(shortcutString) {
  const tokens = String(shortcutString || '')
    .split('+')
    .map((t) => t.trim())
    .filter(Boolean);

  const shortcut = {
    meta: false,
    ctrl: false,
    alt: false,
    shift: false,
    key: 'E'
  };

  for (const token of tokens) {
    const lower = token.toLowerCase();
    if (lower === 'meta' || lower === 'cmd' || lower === 'command') shortcut.meta = true;
    else if (lower === 'ctrl' || lower === 'control') shortcut.ctrl = true;
    else if (lower === 'alt' || lower === 'option') shortcut.alt = true;
    else if (lower === 'shift') shortcut.shift = true;
    else shortcut.key = lower.toUpperCase();
  }

  return shortcut;
}

function serializeShortcut(shortcut) {
  const parts = [];
  if (shortcut.meta) parts.push('Meta');
  if (shortcut.ctrl) parts.push('Ctrl');
  if (shortcut.alt) parts.push('Alt');
  if (shortcut.shift) parts.push('Shift');
  parts.push(shortcut.key.toUpperCase());
  return parts.join('+');
}

function applyToForm(shortcut) {
  metaEl.checked = shortcut.meta;
  ctrlEl.checked = shortcut.ctrl;
  altEl.checked = shortcut.alt;
  shiftEl.checked = shortcut.shift;
  keyEl.value = shortcut.key;
}

async function load() {
  const stored = await chrome.storage.sync.get([SHORTCUT_KEY]);
  const shortcut = parseShortcut(stored[SHORTCUT_KEY] ?? DEFAULT_SHORTCUT);
  applyToForm(shortcut);
}

formEl.addEventListener('submit', async (event) => {
  event.preventDefault();

  const key = keyEl.value.trim();
  if (!key || key.length !== 1) {
    statusEl.textContent = 'Enter a single character key.';
    return;
  }

  const shortcut = {
    meta: metaEl.checked,
    ctrl: ctrlEl.checked,
    alt: altEl.checked,
    shift: shiftEl.checked,
    key
  };

  await chrome.storage.sync.set({
    [SHORTCUT_KEY]: serializeShortcut(shortcut)
  });

  statusEl.textContent = `Saved: ${serializeShortcut(shortcut)}`;
});

load().catch(() => {
  statusEl.textContent = 'Could not load shortcut settings.';
});
EOF
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="exec_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **exec_command**</summary>
```bash
cat > apps/extension/src/options.js <<'EOF'
const SHORTCUT_KEY = 'shortcut';
const DEFAULT_SHORTCUT = 'Meta+Shift+E';

const currentShortcutEl = document.getElementById('current-shortcut');
const recordBtn = document.getElementById('record-btn');
const resetBtn = document.getElementById('reset-btn');
const statusEl = document.getElementById('status');

let isRecording = false;

function normalizeKey(key) {
  if (!key) {
    return '';
  }

  if (key === ' ') {
    return 'Space';
  }

  if (key.length === 1) {
    return key.toUpperCase();
  }

  if (key.startsWith('Arrow')) {
    return key;
  }

  return key[0].toUpperCase() + key.slice(1);
}

function parseShortcut(shortcutString) {
  const tokens = String(shortcutString || '')
    .split('+')
    .map((t) => t.trim())
    .filter(Boolean);

  const shortcut = {
    meta: false,
    ctrl: false,
    alt: false,
    shift: false,
    key: 'E'
  };

  for (const token of tokens) {
    const lower = token.toLowerCase();
    if (lower === 'meta' || lower === 'cmd' || lower === 'command') shortcut.meta = true;
    else if (lower === 'ctrl' || lower === 'control') shortcut.ctrl = true;
    else if (lower === 'alt' || lower === 'option') shortcut.alt = true;
    else if (lower === 'shift') shortcut.shift = true;
    else shortcut.key = normalizeKey(token);
  }

  return shortcut;
}

function serializeShortcut(shortcut) {
  const parts = [];
  if (shortcut.meta) parts.push('Meta');
  if (shortcut.ctrl) parts.push('Ctrl');
  if (shortcut.alt) parts.push('Alt');
  if (shortcut.shift) parts.push('Shift');
  parts.push(normalizeKey(shortcut.key));
  return parts.join('+');
}

function formatShortcutLabel(shortcut) {
  const parts = [];
  if (shortcut.meta) parts.push('Cmd');
  if (shortcut.ctrl) parts.push('Ctrl');
  if (shortcut.alt) parts.push('Alt');
  if (shortcut.shift) parts.push('Shift');
  parts.push(normalizeKey(shortcut.key));
  return parts.join('+');
}

function updateRecordingUi() {
  recordBtn.textContent = isRecording ? 'Press keys now...' : 'Record shortcut';
  resetBtn.disabled = isRecording;
}

async function renderCurrentShortcut() {
  const stored = await chrome.storage.sync.get([SHORTCUT_KEY]);
  const shortcut = parseShortcut(stored[SHORTCUT_KEY] ?? DEFAULT_SHORTCUT);
  currentShortcutEl.textContent = formatShortcutLabel(shortcut);
}

function hasModifier(event) {
  return event.metaKey || event.ctrlKey || event.altKey || event.shiftKey;
}

function isModifierKey(key) {
  return key === 'Meta' || key === 'Control' || key === 'Alt' || key === 'Shift';
}

async function saveShortcut(shortcut) {
  const serialized = serializeShortcut(shortcut);
  await chrome.storage.sync.set({ [SHORTCUT_KEY]: serialized });
  currentShortcutEl.textContent = formatShortcutLabel(shortcut);
  statusEl.textContent = `Saved: ${formatShortcutLabel(shortcut)}`;
}

window.addEventListener('keydown', async (event) => {
  if (!isRecording) {
    return;
  }

  event.preventDefault();

  if (event.key === 'Escape') {
    isRecording = false;
    updateRecordingUi();
    statusEl.textContent = 'Recording canceled.';
    return;
  }

  if (isModifierKey(event.key)) {
    statusEl.textContent = 'Press at least one non-modifier key.';
    return;
  }

  if (!hasModifier(event)) {
    statusEl.textContent = 'Include at least one modifier (Cmd/Ctrl/Alt/Shift).';
    return;
  }

  const shortcut = {
    meta: event.metaKey,
    ctrl: event.ctrlKey,
    alt: event.altKey,
    shift: event.shiftKey,
    key: normalizeKey(event.key)
  };

  isRecording = false;
  updateRecordingUi();
  await saveShortcut(shortcut);
});

recordBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  isRecording = true;
  updateRecordingUi();
  statusEl.textContent = 'Recording... press your preferred shortcut (Esc to cancel).';
});

resetBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
  const shortcut = parseShortcut(DEFAULT_SHORTCUT);
  await chrome.storage.sync.set({ [SHORTCUT_KEY]: DEFAULT_SHORTCUT });
  currentShortcutEl.textContent = formatShortcutLabel(shortcut);
  statusEl.textContent = `Reset to default: ${formatShortcutLabel(shortcut)}`;
});

renderCurrentShortcut().then(updateRecordingUi).catch(() => {
  statusEl.textContent = 'Could not load shortcut settings.';
  updateRecordingUi();
});
EOF
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-26 22:03:04Z)**_

Likely cause was Gmail intercepting key events before our old listener saw them.

I changed the extension to make shortcut handling more reliable:
- Listens in **capture phase** (`document.addEventListener(..., true)`)
- Removed the fragile `event.target.closest(...)` gate
- Triggers from actual active compose detection instead
- Stops propagation once matched

Updated file:
- [content.js](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/apps/extension/src/content.js)

What to do now:
1. Reload extension in `chrome://extensions`.
2. Open Gmail, click into a compose draft, select text.
3. Press your configured shortcut again.

If it still doesn’t fire, tell me the exact shortcut you recorded and I’ll add an on-screen debug indicator showing the key event being captured.

---

     9	export async function rewriteEmail(request, deps = {}) {
    10	  const config = deps.config ?? await loadConfig();
    11	  const styleMarkdown = deps.styleMarkdown ?? await loadStyleMarkdown();
    12	  const parsedRules = parseStyleMarkdown(styleMarkdown);
    13	  const profile = deps.profile ?? await loadProfile();
    14	  const registry = deps.providerRegistry ?? providerRegistry;
    15	
    16	  const mode = normalizeMode(request.mode);
    17	  const mergedRules = mergeStyleRules({
    18	    rules: parsedRules,
    19	    mode,
    20	    profile
    21	  });
    22	
    23	  const rulesPrompt = styleRulesToPrompt(mergedRules);
    24	  const attempts = [];
    25	
    26	  for (const providerName of config.providerOrder) {
    27	    const provider = registry[providerName];
    28	    if (!provider) {
    29	      continue;
    30	    }
    31	
    32	    try {
    33	      const rewrittenText = await provider.rewrite({
    34	        text: request.text,
    35	        mode,
    36	        rulesPrompt,
    37	        timeoutMs: config.timeoutMs
    38	      });
    39	
    40	      if (!deps.skipHistory) {
    41	        await appendHistory({
    42	          ts: new Date().toISOString(),
    43	          mode,
    44	          provider: providerName,
    45	          text: request.text,
    46	          rewrittenText
    47	        });
    48	      }
    49	
    50	      return {
    51	        rewrittenText,
    52	        appliedMode: mode,
    53	        providerUsed: providerName,
    54	        notes: attempts
    55	      };
    56	    } catch (error) {
    57	      attempts.push(`${providerName}: ${error.message}`);
    58	    }
    59	  }
    60	
    61	  throw new Error(`All providers failed. ${attempts.join(' | ')}`);
    62	}
    63	
    64	export async function buildProfile(samples) {
    65	  const profile = buildProfileFromSamples(samples);
    66	  await saveProfile(profile);
    67	  return profile;
    68	}

     1	# EmailBuddy
     2	
     3	EmailBuddy is a macOS-focused toolchain for beautifying Gmail draft text in your personal style.
     4	
     5	## Components
     6	- `apps/extension`: Chrome extension that rewrites draft text from a keyboard shortcut in Gmail compose.
     7	- `apps/companion`: Local HTTP service (`127.0.0.1:48123`) that rewrites text with cloud-first + local fallback.
     8	- `packages/shared`: Style/profile parsing and common helpers.
     9	
    10	## Quick Start
    11	1. Start the companion service:
    12	```bash
    13	npm run dev
    14	```
    15	2. Add cloud keys to macOS Keychain (optional but recommended):
    16	```bash
    17	security add-generic-password -U -a openai_api_key -s emailbuddy -w '<OPENAI_KEY>'
    18	security add-generic-password -U -a anthropic_api_key -s emailbuddy -w '<ANTHROPIC_KEY>'
    19	```
    20	3. Ensure local fallback is available:
    21	```bash
    22	ollama serve
    23	ollama pull llama3.1:8b
    24	```
    25	4. Load extension in Chrome:
    26	- Open `chrome://extensions`
    27	- Enable Developer Mode
    28	- Click **Load unpacked** and select `apps/extension/src`
    29	- In Gmail compose, press your configured shortcut (default: `Cmd+Shift+E`) to beautify selected text (or full draft if nothing is selected).
    30	- Configure the shortcut:
    31	  - Open extension details in `chrome://extensions`
    32	  - Click **Extension options**
    33	  - Click **Record shortcut** and press your preferred key combination
    34	
    35	## Style Configuration
    36	Edit `~/.emailbuddy/STYLE.md` to define mode behavior:
    37	
    38	```md
    39	## mode: casual
    40	do: sound warm and collaborative
    41	avoid: stiff wording
    42	```
    43	
    44	Markdown rules override learned profile traits.
    45	
    46	## API
    47	- `POST /v1/rewrite`
    48	- `POST /v1/profile/samples`
    49	- `GET/PUT /v1/config`
    50	- `POST /v1/secrets`
    51	
    52	## Test and Build
    53	```bash
    54	npm test
    55	npm run build
    56	```
    57	
    58	## Optional: Auto-start Companion at Login
    59	1. Copy and edit [docs/com.emailbuddy.companion.plist](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/docs/com.emailbuddy.companion.plist) (replace `USERNAME` path).
    60	2. Install it:
    61	```bash
    62	cp docs/com.emailbuddy.companion.plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
    63	launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.emailbuddy.companion.plist
    64	```

mkdir -p apps/companion/src/static && cat > apps/companion/src/static/test-ui.html <<'EOF'
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
    <title>EmailBuddy Test UI</title>
    <style>
      body {
        font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif;
        margin: 24px;
        color: #1f1f1f;
      }
      main {
        max-width: 900px;
      }
      textarea {
        width: 100%;
        min-height: 180px;
        padding: 10px;
        border: 1px solid #cfcfcf;
        border-radius: 8px;
        font: 14px/1.5 Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
      }
      .row {
        display: flex;
        gap: 12px;
        margin: 12px 0;
        align-items: center;
      }
      button, select {
        padding: 8px 10px;
        border-radius: 6px;
        border: 1px solid #b6b6b6;
      }
      .status {
        color: #444;
        min-height: 20px;
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <main>
      <h1>EmailBuddy Backend Test UI</h1>
      <p>Use this page to test rewrite behavior without Gmail extension involvement.</p>

      <label for="input">Input</label>
      <textarea id="input" placeholder="Write or paste email text..."></textarea>

      <div class="row">
        <label for="mode">Mode</label>
        <select id="mode">
          <option value="casual">casual</option>
          <option value="polished">polished</option>
          <option value="concise">concise</option>
          <option value="custom">custom</option>
        </select>
        <input id="custom-mode" placeholder="custom mode" style="display:none; padding:8px 10px; border:1px solid #b6b6b6; border-radius:6px;" />
        <button id="rewrite">Rewrite</button>
      </div>

      <p class="status" id="status"></p>

      <label for="output">Output</label>
      <textarea id="output" readonly></textarea>
    </main>

    <script>
      const modeEl = document.getElementById('mode');
      const customEl = document.getElementById('custom-mode');
      const inputEl = document.getElementById('input');
      const outputEl = document.getElementById('output');
      const statusEl = document.getElementById('status');
      const buttonEl = document.getElementById('rewrite');

      modeEl.addEventListener('change', () => {
        customEl.style.display = modeEl.value === 'custom' ? 'inline-block' : 'none';
      });

      buttonEl.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        const text = inputEl.value.trim();
        if (!text) {
          statusEl.textContent = 'Input is empty.';
          return;
        }

        const mode = modeEl.value === 'custom' ? (customEl.value.trim() || 'casual') : modeEl.value;

        buttonEl.disabled = true;
        statusEl.textContent = 'Rewriting...';

        try {
          const res = await fetch('/v1/rewrite', {
            method: 'POST',
            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
            body: JSON.stringify({ text, mode })
          });

          const body = await res.json();
          if (!res.ok) {
            throw new Error(body.error || 'Rewrite failed');
          }

          outputEl.value = body.rewrittenText;
          statusEl.textContent = `Done via ${body.providerUsed}.`;
        } catch (error) {
          statusEl.textContent = error.message;
        } finally {
          buttonEl.disabled = false;
        }
      });
    </script>
  </body>
</html>
EOF

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-26 22:10:59Z)**_

Implemented both requests.

### What I added

- Structured JSON logging in companion server:
  - Request start/end with `requestId`, status, duration
  - Rewrite pipeline events:
    - `rewrite.start`
    - `rewrite.provider.attempt`
    - `rewrite.provider.success`
    - `rewrite.provider.error`
- Simple backend test UI served by companion:
  - `GET /test-ui` (also available at `/`)
  - Lets you paste text, choose mode, call `/v1/rewrite`, and view output

Changed files:
- [index.js](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/apps/companion/src/index.js)
- [rewrite.js](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/apps/companion/src/rewrite.js)
- [test-ui.html](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/apps/companion/src/static/test-ui.html)
- [README.md](/Users/isauerzapf/Development/emailbuddy/README.md)

### How to use now

1. Start companion:
   - `npm run dev`
2. Open test UI:
   - `http://127.0.0.1:48123/test-ui`
3. Watch terminal logs while testing.

### Why this helps your Gmail shortcut issue

- If you press the Gmail shortcut and **no new `http.request.start` for `/v1/rewrite` appears**, the issue is extension-side (shortcut capture/injection).
- If logs **do appear** but request fails, it’s backend/provider/config related.

### Verified

- `npm test` passes.
- `npm run build` passes.
- I manually hit `/test-ui` and `/v1/rewrite`; logs show full request/provider traces.

If you want, next I can add a tiny extension-side debug overlay that shows “shortcut captured” before the network call, so we can pinpoint failure to keyboard vs network in one attempt.

     9	export async function rewriteEmail(request, deps = {}) {
    10	  const logger = deps.logger ?? (() => {});

    12	  const config = deps.config ?? await loadConfig();

    20	    rules: parsedRules,
    21	    mode,
    22	    profile
    23	  });
    24	
    25	  const rulesPrompt = styleRulesToPrompt(mergedRules);
    26	  const attempts = [];

    28	    requestId,

    31	    providers: config.providerOrder
    32	  });
    33	
    34	  for (const providerName of config.providerOrder) {
    35	    const provider = registry[providerName];
    36	    if (!provider) {
    37	      logger('rewrite.provider.missing', { requestId, providerName });
    38	      continue;
    39	    }
    40	
    41	    try {
    42	      const startedAt = Date.now();
    43	      logger('rewrite.provider.attempt', { requestId, providerName });
    44	      const rewrittenText = await provider.rewrite({
    45	        text: request.text,
    46	        mode,
    47	        rulesPrompt,
    48	        timeoutMs: config.timeoutMs

    55	        outputLength: rewrittenText.length

    57	
    58	      if (!deps.skipHistory) {

    70	        appliedMode: mode,

    72	        notes: attempts

cat > apps/companion/src/config.js <<'EOF'
import { appendFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
import {
  CONFIG_PATH,
  HISTORY_PATH,
  PROFILE_PATH,
  STYLE_PATH,
  ensureEmailBuddyDir,
  readJson,
  readText,
  writeJson
} from './file-utils.js';

export const ALLOWED_PROVIDERS = ['ollama', 'openai', 'anthropic'];

export const CONFIG_SCHEMA = {
  defaults: {
    host: '127.0.0.1',
    port: 48123,
    providerOrder: ['ollama', 'openai', 'anthropic'],
    history: {
      enabled: false
    },
    timeoutMs: 12000
  },
  constraints: {
    timeoutMs: {
      min: 1000,
      max: 60000
    },
    port: {
      min: 1,
      max: 65535
    },
    providers: ALLOWED_PROVIDERS
  }
};

const DEFAULT_STYLE = `# EmailBuddy Style Configuration\n\n## global\n\ndo: keep language clear and natural for non-native English writer\navoid: overly formal phrases and corporate jargon\n\n## mode: casual\n\ndo: sound warm and collaborative\n\n## mode: polished\n\ndo: improve grammar and sentence flow\n\n## mode: concise\n\ndo: reduce unnecessary words\n`;

function normalizeProviderOrder(order) {
  if (!Array.isArray(order)) {
    throw new Error('providerOrder must be an array');
  }

  const normalized = order.map((p) => String(p).trim().toLowerCase()).filter(Boolean);
  if (!normalized.length) {
    throw new Error('providerOrder must include at least one provider');
  }

  const seen = new Set();
  for (const provider of normalized) {
    if (!ALLOWED_PROVIDERS.includes(provider)) {
      throw new Error(`Unsupported provider in providerOrder: ${provider}`);
    }
    if (seen.has(provider)) {
      throw new Error(`providerOrder contains duplicate provider: ${provider}`);
    }
    seen.add(provider);
  }

  return normalized;
}

function normalizeTimeout(timeoutMs) {
  const num = Number(timeoutMs);
  if (!Number.isFinite(num) || !Number.isInteger(num)) {
    throw new Error('timeoutMs must be an integer');
  }

  if (num < CONFIG_SCHEMA.constraints.timeoutMs.min || num > CONFIG_SCHEMA.constraints.timeoutMs.max) {
    throw new Error(`timeoutMs must be between ${CONFIG_SCHEMA.constraints.timeoutMs.min} and ${CONFIG_SCHEMA.constraints.timeoutMs.max}`);
  }

  return num;
}

function normalizeHost(host) {
  const value = String(host ?? '').trim();
  if (!value) {
    throw new Error('host must be a non-empty string');
  }
  return value;
}

function normalizePort(port) {
  const num = Number(port);
  if (!Number.isFinite(num) || !Number.isInteger(num)) {
    throw new Error('port must be an integer');
  }

  if (num < CONFIG_SCHEMA.constraints.port.min || num > CONFIG_SCHEMA.constraints.port.max) {
    throw new Error(`port must be between ${CONFIG_SCHEMA.constraints.port.min} and ${CONFIG_SCHEMA.constraints.port.max}`);
  }

  return num;
}

function normalizeHistory(history) {
  if (typeof history !== 'object' || history === null) {
    throw new Error('history must be an object');
  }

  if (typeof history.enabled !== 'boolean') {
    throw new Error('history.enabled must be boolean');
  }

  return { enabled: history.enabled };
}

export function validateConfig(config) {
  return {
    host: normalizeHost(config.host),
    port: normalizePort(config.port),
    providerOrder: normalizeProviderOrder(config.providerOrder),
    history: normalizeHistory(config.history),
    timeoutMs: normalizeTimeout(config.timeoutMs)
  };
}

function mergeConfig(baseConfig, patchConfig) {
  return {
    ...baseConfig,
    ...patchConfig,
    history: {
      ...baseConfig.history,
      ...(patchConfig.history ?? {})
    }
  };
}

export async function loadConfig() {
  await ensureEmailBuddyDir();
  const raw = await readJson(CONFIG_PATH, CONFIG_SCHEMA.defaults);
  const merged = mergeConfig(CONFIG_SCHEMA.defaults, raw);
  return validateConfig(merged);
}

export async function saveConfig(nextConfig) {
  const current = await loadConfig();
  const merged = mergeConfig(current, nextConfig ?? {});
  const validated = validateConfig(merged);

  await writeJson(CONFIG_PATH, validated);
  return validated;
}

export async function getConfigSchema() {
  return CONFIG_SCHEMA;
}

export async function loadStyleMarkdown() {
  return readText(STYLE_PATH, DEFAULT_STYLE);
}

export async function loadProfile() {
  return readJson(PROFILE_PATH, null);
}

export async function saveProfile(profile) {
  await writeJson(PROFILE_PATH, profile);
  return profile;
}

export async function appendHistory(item) {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  if (!config.history.enabled) {
    return;
  }

  await ensureEmailBuddyDir();
  await appendFile(HISTORY_PATH, `${JSON.stringify(item)}\n`, 'utf8');
}
EOF

cat > apps/companion/src/static/test-ui.html <<'EOF'
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
    <title>EmailBuddy Test UI</title>
    <style>
      body {
        font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif;
        margin: 24px;
        color: #1f1f1f;
      }
      main {
        max-width: 980px;
      }
      h2 {
        margin-top: 26px;
      }
      textarea {
        width: 100%;
        min-height: 180px;
        padding: 10px;
        border: 1px solid #cfcfcf;
        border-radius: 8px;
        font: 14px/1.5 Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
      }
      .row {
        display: flex;
        gap: 12px;
        margin: 12px 0;
        align-items: center;
        flex-wrap: wrap;
      }
      button, select, input[type="number"], input[type="password"], input[type="text"] {
        padding: 8px 10px;
        border-radius: 6px;
        border: 1px solid #b6b6b6;
      }
      .status {
        color: #444;
        min-height: 20px;
      }
      .panel {
        border: 1px solid #dedede;
        border-radius: 10px;
        padding: 12px;
        background: #fafafa;
      }
      .providers {
        list-style: none;
        padding: 0;
        margin: 8px 0;
      }
      .providers li {
        display: flex;
        align-items: center;
        gap: 8px;
        margin: 6px 0;
      }
      .provider-name {
        width: 110px;
        font-weight: 600;
      }
      .hint {
        color: #666;
        font-size: 13px;
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <main>
      <h1>EmailBuddy Backend Test UI</h1>
      <p>Use this page to test rewrite behavior and backend settings without Gmail extension involvement.</p>

      <h2>Rewrite Test</h2>
      <label for="input">Input</label>
      <textarea id="input" placeholder="Write or paste email text..."></textarea>

      <div class="row">
        <label for="mode">Mode</label>
        <select id="mode">
          <option value="casual">casual</option>
          <option value="polished">polished</option>
          <option value="concise">concise</option>
          <option value="custom">custom</option>
        </select>
        <input id="custom-mode" placeholder="custom mode" style="display:none;" />
        <button id="rewrite">Rewrite</button>
      </div>

      <p class="status" id="rewrite-status"></p>

      <label for="output">Output</label>
      <textarea id="output" readonly></textarea>

      <h2>Settings</h2>
      <section class="panel">
        <p class="hint">Companion config is the source of truth for both this page and extension options.</p>

        <h3>Provider Order</h3>
        <ul id="provider-order" class="providers"></ul>

        <h3>Runtime</h3>
        <div class="row">
          <label for="timeout-ms">Timeout (ms)</label>
          <input id="timeout-ms" type="number" min="1000" max="60000" step="100" />
          <label><input id="history-enabled" type="checkbox" /> Enable history logging</label>
          <button id="save-config">Save config</button>
        </div>

        <p class="status" id="config-status"></p>

        <h3>API Keys</h3>
        <div class="row">
          <label for="openai-key">OpenAI key</label>
          <input id="openai-key" type="password" placeholder="sk-..." />
          <button id="save-openai">Save OpenAI key</button>
        </div>
        <div class="row">
          <label for="anthropic-key">Anthropic key</label>
          <input id="anthropic-key" type="password" placeholder="sk-ant-..." />
          <button id="save-anthropic">Save Anthropic key</button>
        </div>

        <p class="status" id="secrets-status"></p>
      </section>
    </main>

    <script>
      const modeEl = document.getElementById('mode');
      const customEl = document.getElementById('custom-mode');
      const inputEl = document.getElementById('input');
      const outputEl = document.getElementById('output');
      const rewriteStatusEl = document.getElementById('rewrite-status');
      const rewriteBtn = document.getElementById('rewrite');

      const providerListEl = document.getElementById('provider-order');
      const timeoutEl = document.getElementById('timeout-ms');
      const historyEl = document.getElementById('history-enabled');
      const saveConfigBtn = document.getElementById('save-config');
      const configStatusEl = document.getElementById('config-status');

      const openaiKeyEl = document.getElementById('openai-key');
      const anthropicKeyEl = document.getElementById('anthropic-key');
      const saveOpenAiBtn = document.getElementById('save-openai');
      const saveAnthropicBtn = document.getElementById('save-anthropic');
      const secretsStatusEl = document.getElementById('secrets-status');

      let providerOrder = [];

      function renderProviderOrder() {
        providerListEl.innerHTML = '';

        providerOrder.forEach((provider, index) => {
          const li = document.createElement('li');
          const name = document.createElement('span');
          name.className = 'provider-name';
          name.textContent = `${index + 1}. ${provider}`;

          const up = document.createElement('button');
          up.type = 'button';
          up.textContent = 'Up';
          up.disabled = index === 0;
          up.addEventListener('click', () => {
            const next = [...providerOrder];
            [next[index - 1], next[index]] = [next[index], next[index - 1]];
            providerOrder = next;
            renderProviderOrder();
          });

          const down = document.createElement('button');
          down.type = 'button';
          down.textContent = 'Down';
          down.disabled = index === providerOrder.length - 1;
          down.addEventListener('click', () => {
            const next = [...providerOrder];
            [next[index + 1], next[index]] = [next[index], next[index + 1]];
            providerOrder = next;
            renderProviderOrder();
          });

          li.append(name, up, down);
          providerListEl.appendChild(li);
        });
      }

      async function refreshSecretsStatus() {
        const res = await fetch('/v1/secrets/status');
        const body = await res.json();
        if (!res.ok) {
          throw new Error(body.error || 'Could not load secrets status');
        }

        secretsStatusEl.textContent = `OpenAI: ${body.openaiConfigured ? 'configured' : 'missing'} | Anthropic: ${body.anthropicConfigured ? 'configured' : 'missing'}`;
      }

      async function loadSettings() {
        const [configRes, schemaRes] = await Promise.all([
          fetch('/v1/config'),
          fetch('/v1/config/schema')
        ]);

        const configBody = await configRes.json();
        const schemaBody = await schemaRes.json();

        if (!configRes.ok) {
          throw new Error(configBody.error || 'Could not load config');
        }
        if (!schemaRes.ok) {
          throw new Error(schemaBody.error || 'Could not load config schema');
        }

        providerOrder = [...configBody.providerOrder];
        timeoutEl.min = String(schemaBody.constraints.timeoutMs.min);
        timeoutEl.max = String(schemaBody.constraints.timeoutMs.max);
        timeoutEl.value = configBody.timeoutMs;
        historyEl.checked = configBody.history.enabled;
        renderProviderOrder();
      }

      async function saveConfig() {
        saveConfigBtn.disabled = true;
        configStatusEl.textContent = 'Saving config...';
        try {
          const res = await fetch('/v1/config', {
            method: 'PUT',
            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
            body: JSON.stringify({
              providerOrder,
              timeoutMs: Number(timeoutEl.value),
              history: { enabled: historyEl.checked }
            })
          });

          const body = await res.json();
          if (!res.ok) {
            throw new Error(body.error || 'Could not save config');
          }

          providerOrder = [...body.providerOrder];
          timeoutEl.value = body.timeoutMs;
          historyEl.checked = body.history.enabled;
          renderProviderOrder();
          configStatusEl.textContent = 'Config saved.';
        } catch (error) {
          configStatusEl.textContent = error.message;
        } finally {
          saveConfigBtn.disabled = false;
        }
      }

      async function saveSecret(account, value) {
        const res = await fetch('/v1/secrets', {
          method: 'POST',
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
          body: JSON.stringify({ account, value })
        });

        const body = await res.json();
        if (!res.ok) {
          throw new Error(body.error || `Could not save secret for ${account}`);
        }
      }

      modeEl.addEventListener('change', () => {
        customEl.style.display = modeEl.value === 'custom' ? 'inline-block' : 'none';
      });

      rewriteBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        const text = inputEl.value.trim();
        if (!text) {
          rewriteStatusEl.textContent = 'Input is empty.';
          return;
        }

        const mode = modeEl.value === 'custom' ? (customEl.value.trim() || 'casual') : modeEl.value;

        rewriteBtn.disabled = true;
        rewriteStatusEl.textContent = 'Rewriting...';

        try {
          const res = await fetch('/v1/rewrite', {
            method: 'POST',
            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
            body: JSON.stringify({ text, mode })
          });

          const body = await res.json();
          if (!res.ok) {
            throw new Error(body.error || 'Rewrite failed');
          }

          outputEl.value = body.rewrittenText;
          rewriteStatusEl.textContent = `Done via ${body.providerUsed}.`;
        } catch (error) {
          rewriteStatusEl.textContent = error.message;
        } finally {
          rewriteBtn.disabled = false;
        }
      });

      saveConfigBtn.addEventListener('click', saveConfig);

      saveOpenAiBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        const value = openaiKeyEl.value.trim();
        if (!value) {
          secretsStatusEl.textContent = 'OpenAI key is empty.';
          return;
        }

        saveOpenAiBtn.disabled = true;
        try {
          await saveSecret('openai_api_key', value);
          openaiKeyEl.value = '';
          await refreshSecretsStatus();
        } catch (error) {
          secretsStatusEl.textContent = error.message;
        } finally {
          saveOpenAiBtn.disabled = false;
        }
      });

      saveAnthropicBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
        const value = anthropicKeyEl.value.trim();
        if (!value) {
          secretsStatusEl.textContent = 'Anthropic key is empty.';
          return;
        }

        saveAnthropicBtn.disabled = true;
        try {
          await saveSecret('anthropic_api_key', value);
          anthropicKeyEl.value = '';
          await refreshSecretsStatus();
        } catch (error) {
          secretsStatusEl.textContent = error.message;
        } finally {
          saveAnthropicBtn.disabled = false;
        }
      });

      Promise.all([loadSettings(), refreshSecretsStatus()]).catch((error) => {
        configStatusEl.textContent = error.message;
      });
    </script>
  </body>
</html>
EOF

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
